qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Digital link token enumeration allows unauthorized access to purchased digital products
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes an authorization bypass in OrdersController#show for viewing completed guest orders without token validation. This finding describes a different vulnerability in DigitalsController for digital download access via token enumeration. Different endpoint (/orders vs /api/v2/storefront/digitals/download), different controller, different vulnerable operation (missing token requirement for guest orders vs missing user identity validation for digital links), different root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-p6pv-q7rc-g4h9) describes an authorization bypass in Spree Commerce OrdersController#show that allows unauthenticated users to view completed guest orders by Order ID alone without requiring the order token. The root cause is the authorize_access method returning true for guest orders (user_id.nil?) without token validation. The single qualifying finding (digital_link_token_enumeration) describes a different vulnerability in a different controller (DigitalsController), different endpoint (/api/v2/storefront/digitals/download), and different root cause (missing user identity validation for digital link tokens). While both are authorization bypass vulnerabilities in the same codebase, they affect different components with different vulnerable code paths.
evidence: Advisory root cause: authorize_access returns true when @order.user_id.nil? without checking params[:token]
Advisory endpoint: GET /orders/<ORDER-ID> for viewing guest order details
Finding endpoint: GET /api/v2/storefront/digitals/download?token={token} for digital downloads
Finding root cause: DigitalLink.authorize! validates token and access limits but not user identity
Different vulnerable functions, different authorization logic, different attack surface
